segments.text: And the word of the LORD came to me, saying,
Son of man, set your face against Jerusalem and preach against the sanctuaries. Prophesy against the land of Israel
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: and tell her that this is what the LORD says: ‘I am against you, and I will draw My sword from its sheath and cut off from you both the righteous and the wicked.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Because I will cut off both the righteous and the wicked, My sword will be unsheathed against everyone from south to north.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Then all flesh will know that I, the LORD, have taken My sword from its sheath, not to return it again.’
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: But you, son of man, groan! Groan before their eyes with a broken heart and bitter grief.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And when they ask, ‘Why are you groaning?’ you are to say, ‘Because of the news that is coming. Every heart will melt, and every hand will go limp. Every spirit will faint, and every knee will turn to water.’ Yes, it is coming and it will surely happen, declares the Lord GOD.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Again the word of the LORD came to me, saying,

Son of man, prophesy and tell them that this is what the Lord says:
‘A sword, a sword, sharpened and polished—
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: it is sharpened for the slaughter, polished to flash like lightning! Should we rejoice in the scepter of My son? The sword despises every such stick.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: The sword is appointed to be polished, to be grasped in the hand. It is sharpened and polished, to be placed in the hand of the slayer.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Cry out and wail, O son of man, for the sword is wielded against My people; it is against all the princes of Israel! They are tossed to the sword with My people; therefore strike your thigh.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Surely testing will come! And what if even the scepter, which the sword despises, does not continue?’declares the Lord GOD.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text:
‘So then, son of man, prophesy and strike your hands together. Let the sword strike two times, even three. It is a sword that slays, a sword of great slaughter
closing in on every side!
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: So that their hearts may melt and many may stumble, I have appointed at all their gatesa sword for slaughter. Yes, it is ready to flash like lightning; it is drawn for slaughter.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text:
Slash to the right; set your blade to the left—
wherever your blade is directed.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I too will strike My hands together, and I will satisfy My wrath.’I, the LORD, have spoken.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Then the word of the LORD came to me, saying,
Now you, son of man, mark out two roads for the sword of the king of Babylon to take, both starting from the same land. And make a signpost where the road branches off to each city.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Mark out one road for the sword to come against Rabbah of the Ammonites, and another against Judah into fortified Jerusalem.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: For the king of Babylon stands at the fork in the road, at the junction of the two roads, to seek an omen: He shakes the arrows, he consults the idols, he examines the liver.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: In his right hand appears the portent for Jerusalem, where he is to set up battering rams, to call for the slaughter, to lift a battle cry, to direct the battering rams against the gates, to build a ramp, and to erect a siege wall.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: It will seem like a false omen to the eyes of those who have sworn allegiance to him, but it will draw attention to their guilt and take them captive.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Therefore this is what the Lord GOD says: ‘Because you have drawn attention to your guilt, exposing your transgressions, so that your sins are revealed in all your deeds—because you have come to remembrance—you shall be taken in hand.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And you, O profane and wicked prince of Israel, the day has come for your final punishment.’
segments.speaker: God

segments.text:
This is what the Lord GOD says:
‘Remove the turban, and take off the crown. Things will not remain as they are: Exalt the lowly
and bring low the exalted.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text:
A ruin, a ruin, I will make it a ruin! And it will not be restoreduntil the arrival of Him to whom it belongs,
to whom I have assigned the right of judgment.’
segments.speaker: God

segments.text:
Now prophesy, son of man, and declare that this is what the Lord GOD says concerning the Ammonites and their contempt:
‘A sword! A swordis drawn for slaughter, polished to consume, to flash like lightning—
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: while they offer false visions for you and lying divinations about you—to be placed on the necksof the wicked who are slain, whose day has come, the time of their final punishment.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text:
Return the sword to its sheath!
In the place where you were created, in the land of your origin,
I will judge you.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I will pour out My anger upon you; I will breathe the fire of My fury against you; I will hand you over to brutal men, skilled in destruction.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You will be fuel for the fire. Your blood will stain your own land. You will not be remembered, for I, the LORD, have spoken.’
segments.speaker: God
